channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: You Asked—We Answered: Inside the Agent 365 AMA
item.description: If you missed our live Agent 365 demo and 'Ask Microsoft Anything' AMA—or want to revisit the discussion—this replay is worth your time. During the session, we walked through how Agent 365 brings visibility, security, and governance to the rapidly expanding world of AI agents—across both Microsoft...
item.dc:creator: Nichole_Peterson

item.title: Kubernetes v1.36: Advancing Workload-Aware Scheduling
item.description: AI/ML and batch workloads introduce unique scheduling challenges that go beyond simple Pod-by-Pod scheduling. In Kubernetes v1.35, we introduced the first tranche of workload-aware scheduling improvements, featuring the foundational Workload API alongside basic gang scheduling support built on a...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: NVIDIA Dynamo on AKS - Autoscaling LLM Inference
item.description: Large Language Models (LLMs) are rapidly moving from experimentation to production. While training often gets the spotlight, inference at scale is where real-world complexity emerges—especially when performance, cost, and user experience must all be balanced under unpredictable traffic. In this...
item.dc:creator: maljazaery

item.title: What’s New in Microsoft 365 Copilot | April 2026
item.description: Welcome to the April 2026 edition of What's new in Microsoft 365 Copilot! Every month, we highlight new features and enhancements to keep Microsoft 365 admins up to date with Copilot features that help your users be more productive and efficient in the apps they use every day. Let’s take a closer...
item.dc:creator: Seth_Patton

item.title: Start Copilot cloud agent tasks via the REST API
item.description: Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise users can programmatically start Copilot cloud agent tasks with the new Agent tasks REST API, available in public preview. Copilot cloud agent works in the… The post Start Copilot cloud agent tasks via the REST API appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Introducing Azure Container Apps Express!
item.description: Three years ago, a 15-second cold start was industry-leading. Today, developers and AI agents expect sub-second. The speed bar has moved, and the tooling needs to move with it. After running Azure Container Apps for years, we've learned something important: for most developers, the ACA environment...
item.dc:creator: simonjj

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Container Apps Express
item.description: Azure Container Apps Express is now available in public preview. It is the simplest and fastest way to launch and scale powerful applications on Azure, from zero to hyperscale, without infrastructure decisions. It represents the first Azure compute platfo
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Announcing the general availability of Azure Integrated Hardware Security Module
item.description: Today we are excited to release general availability for Azure Integrated HSM for AMD v7 Virtual Machines. Azure Integrated HSM is a hardware security module (HSM) cache and crypto offload designed to enhance the security and performance of cryptographic operations in virtual machines. For...
item.dc:creator: simranparkhe

item.title: .NET MAUI Moves to CoreCLR in .NET 11
item.description: .NET MAUI apps on Android, iOS, and Mac Catalyst now run on CoreCLR by default in .NET 11, unifying the runtime across all of .NET. The post .NET MAUI Moves to CoreCLR in .NET 11 appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Ortinau

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.120
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.120 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available
item.description: GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) 3.21 enhances deployment efficiency, monitoring capabilities, code security, and policy management. Here are a few highlights in the 3.21 release: Organization custom properties are now generally… The post GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Secure Medallion Architecture Pattern on Azure Databricks (Part II)
item.description: Disclaimer: The views in this article are my own and do not represent Microsoft or Databricks. This article is part of a series focused on deploying a secure Medallion Architecture. The series follows a top-down approach , beginning with a high-level architectural perspective and gradually drilling...
item.dc:creator: mscagliola

item.title: Introducing Grok 4.3 on Microsoft Foundry: Latest Generation Agentic Capabilities
item.description: Customers building advanced AI systems increasingly need models that can reason deeply, act autonomously, and integrate reliably into real‑world workflows—all without compromising on governance or cost efficiency. Grok 4.3, xAI’s latest flagship model, is now available in Microsoft Foundry, giving...
item.dc:creator: Naomi Moneypenny

item.title: Agent Skills in Visual Studio: Teach Copilot How Your Team Works
item.description: Visual Studio now supports Agent Skills, which are reusable instruction sets that teach Copilot agents how to handle specific tasks like running a build pipeline, generating boilerplate, or following your team’s coding standards. Define a skill once, and the agent applies it automatically whenever...
item.dc:creator: Simona Liao

item.title: Dungeons & Desktops: 10 roguelikes that never die (because their communities won’t let them)
item.description: Roguelikes don’t die. They fork, mutate, get argued over, rewritten, abandoned, and revived again. Sometimes all at once. The post Dungeons & Desktops: 10 roguelikes that never die (because their communities won’t let them) appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lee Reilly

item.title: How Workday and Microsoft are redefining the agentic workplace
item.description: The modern employee is currently paying a "fragmentation tax." Across a typical workday, the average professional navigates over 200 disparate apps and systems. Every time they switch from a collaborative space like Microsoft Teams to a domain-specific tool to check a PTO balance or a tax form,...
item.dc:creator: Srini_Raghavan_Msft

item.title: v1.16.0-alpha20260513
item.description: 1.16.0-alpha20260513 (May 13, 2026) NEW FEATURES: Store PlannedPrivate data for providers (#37986) New store block in terraform_data that can handle ephemeral and sensitive values (#38298) Providers can now use nested blocks as computed values (#38305) We now produce builds for Linux s390x (zLinux)...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: How to Test AI Agents with LangSmith: A Complete Guide
item.description: Testing AI agents is crucial for ensuring reliability and accuracy in production.Evaluation is a technique to evaluate your agents. Different type of evaluation are # Evaluation Type 1 Task Success (Pass / Fail) 2 Instruction Adherence 3 Correctness / Accuracy 4 Relevance 5 Groundedness...
item.dc:creator: syedarshad

item.title: v1.15.3
item.description: 1.15.3 (May 13, 2026) BUG FIXES: stacks: Fixed a bug that prevented migrating resources under multiple layers of module nesting with implicit provider configuration. (#38528) cloud backend will now forward -generate-config-out flag usage to query create request (#38539) Fix crash during provider...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: Announcing the General Availability of the Next Generation of Azure Boost
item.description: Starting May 7th, 2026, the new Esv7, Dsv7, and Dlsv7 virtual machines are generally available — and underneath them is a fundamentally new generation of Azure Boost. Not an incremental refresh. A platform that took over five years to build, with custom ASIC-hardened logic, a new network adapter,...
item.dc:creator: Max_Uritsky

item.title: Give Your AI Agent Eyes: Browser-Harness Meets Playwright Workspaces Remote Browsers
item.description: What happens when you hand a coding agent a real browser — not a mock, not an API wrapper, but a full Chromium instance running in the cloud? It shops for you. It scrapes for you. It navigates JavaScript-heavy SPAs that would make any REST-based scraper weep. And it does it across 10+ parallel...
item.dc:creator: NandiniMuralidharan

item.title: Introducing cert-manager for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes: now in Public Preview
item.description: Today we’re releasing a public preview of cert-manager for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes. It’s an Arc extension that automates TLS certificate and trust bundle management for edge Kubernetes clusters. If you’re running Kubernetes at the edge: in factories, retail stores, remote sites, you’ve...
item.dc:creator: NicolasMondada

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana in Public, Government (Fairfax) and China
item.description: Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana are generally available, bringing the power of Grafana’s open and composable visualization platform directly into the Azure Portal. This capability enables developers and operators to create, edit, and share Grafana d
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Accelerating detection engineering using AI-assisted synthetic attack logs generation
item.description: What if you could generate realistic attack telemetry on demand? Explore research methods that translate attacker behaviors (TTPs) into synthetic logs that can trigger detections at scale and without sensitive data. The post Accelerating detection engineering using AI-assisted synthetic attack logs...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: .NET 11 Preview 4 is now available!
item.description: Find out about the new features in .NET 11 Preview 4 across the .NET runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and more! The post .NET 11 Preview 4 is now available! appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: .NET Team

item.title: Private Networking and Inference in Microsoft Foundry: Architecture Impact on Enterprise AI
item.description: Enterprise adoption of generative AI rarely fails because of model quality alone. More often, it fails at the intersection of network isolation, dependency reachability, and operational reality – especially in inference-driven applications such as retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and enterprise...
item.dc:creator: marcopunio

item.title: Defense at AI speed: Microsoft’s new multi-model agentic security system tops leading industry benchmark
item.description: Today Microsoft is announcing a major step forward in AI-powered cyber defense: a new multi-model agentic scanning harness (codenamed MDASH). The post Defense at AI speed: Microsoft’s new multi-model agentic security system tops leading industry benchmark appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Taesoo Kim

item.title: Build Smarter, Simpler IoT Messaging with Azure Event Grid MQTT Broker
item.description: Why this matters for modern IoT solutions As IoT solutions grow, teams need messaging that is reliable, scalable, and simple enough to operate across devices, apps, and cloud services. Azure Event Grid MQTT broker helps organizations connect devices and applications using standard MQTT patterns...
item.dc:creator: Connected-Seth

item.title: A New Chapter for Realtime AI: Reasoning, Translation, and Real-Time Transcription
item.description: Voice can be one of the most direct and productive interfaces for AI — enabling customer support agents that may resolve issues without a single keystroke, live multilingual communication that can take on language barriers as conversations happen, and voice assistants capable of reasoning through...
item.dc:creator: Naomi Moneypenny

item.title: Copilot code review: Comment experience improvements
item.description: Copilot code review comments are now easier to scan and act on. Available to all users opted into the new pull requests experience, grouped suggestions, severity levels, and an updated… The post Copilot code review: Comment experience improvements appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: April reports are now available to prepare for usage-based billing
item.description: Starting today, you can download your usage report to see how April’s GitHub Copilot activity translates into AI credits. The new billing unit of AI credits is going live on… The post April reports are now available to prepare for usage-based billing appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: .NET and .NET Framework May 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description: A recap of the latest servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework for May 2026. The post .NET and .NET Framework May 2026 servicing releases updates appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rahul Bhandari (MSFT), Tara Overfield

item.title: Kubernetes v1.36: PSI Metrics for Kubernetes Graduates to GA
item.description: Since its original implementation in the Linux kernel in 2018, Pressure Stall Information (PSI) has provided users with the high-fidelity signals needed to identify resource saturation before it becomes an outage. Unlike traditional utilization metrics, PSI tells the story of tasks stalled and time...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Update: 99.99% uptime for all Azure Service Bus Premium namespaces in Availability Zone regions
item.description: Starting May 1, 2026, all Azure Service Bus Premium namespaces deployed in regions with Availability Zone support now qualify for a 99.99% uptime SLA. Premium is the tier customers choose for their most important workloads, and this update brings the SLA
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Confidential computing for Azure Service Bus Premium
item.description: Confidential computing for Azure Service Bus Premium is now generally available in Korea Central and UAE North. This capability enables Service Bus to process messages inside hardware-based trusted execution environments (TEEs), adding protection for data
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: From Prompt to Production: Open in VS Code for Terraform in Azure Copilot
item.description: We’re excited to introduce a new step in the Terraform on Azure experience: Open in VS Code, now available directly from Azure Copilot in the Azure Portal. This capability helps you move seamlessly from AI‑generated Terraform code to real Azure deployments - within a connected, guided workflow...
item.dc:creator: Jingwei_Wang

item.title: CodeQL 2.25.4 adds Swift 6.3.1 support, improvements to C# and Java, and more
item.description: CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.25.4, which adds Swift 6.3.1 support, improves… The post CodeQL 2.25.4 adds Swift 6.3.1 support, improvements to C# and Java, and more appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Copilot individual plans: Introducing flex allotments in Pro and Pro+, and a new Max plan
item.description: Starting June 1, our lineup of individual plans will update based on your feedback. The post GitHub Copilot individual plans: Introducing flex allotments in Pro and Pro+, and a new Max plan appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Virtual Network Manager rule impact analyzer
item.description: Azure Virtual Network Manager rule impact analyzer is now generally available, empowering you to simulate the impact of your security admin rules on your virtual networks before deploying your rules. With this capability, you can simulate the effects of y
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Sentinel TI - improved pattern parsing & revoke reliability
item.description: We’ve rolled out two important improvements to enhance accuracy and control in pattern-based workflows: Revoke fix: Resolved an issue where revoke actions were not consistently applied, ensuring changes now take effect reliably and as intended. Support fo
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Foundry Local 1.1: Live Transcription, Embeddings, and Responses API
item.description: Foundry Local 1.1 adds live transcription, embeddings, Responses API, WebGPU plugin, and download cancellation. The post Foundry Local 1.1: Live Transcription, Embeddings, and Responses API appeared first on Microsoft Foundry Blog.
item.dc:creator: samkemp

item.title: (Co)work in Progress
item.description: A complete record of milestones, launches, and newest innovations. Give Microsoft 365 Copilot Cowork your team's playbook. Plugins bring your organization's skills and knowledge into one workflow so Cowork can help support teams work the way they already do. Try Copilot Cowork in Frontier In plain...
item.dc:creator: ilyagrebnov

item.title: Synchronous SBOM API deprecated
item.description: Following the recent release of the new Asynchronous SBOM REST API, the older, synchronous API is deprecated and slated for removal in six months, on November 13, 2026. If your… The post Synchronous SBOM API deprecated appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks
item.description: Read how to protect consumer websites and defend against modern DDoS attacks with layered security, resilient architecture, and graceful service degradation. The post Defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Kumar Srinivasamurthy

item.title: Driving Efficiency in Modern System Engineering with AI Agents
item.description: Authors: Pushpendra Kumar, Chockalingam A, Smitha Kashyap, Shilpi Gupta Overview As platforms become more complex and release cycles accelerate, traditional engineering workflows are beginning to show their limits. Engineers are spending increasing amounts of time triaging duplicate issues,...
item.dc:creator: PushpendraKumar

item.title: Dungeons & Desktops: Building a procedurally generated roguelike with GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: Learn how one Hubber used GitHub Copilot CLI to build an extension that turns any codebase into a unique, roguelike dungeon. The post Dungeons & Desktops: Building a procedurally generated roguelike with GitHub Copilot CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lee Reilly

item.title: Undermining the trust boundary: Investigating a stealthy intrusion through third-party compromise
item.description: Microsoft Incident Response investigated an attack operated through legitimate and trusted administrative mechanisms to blend seamlessly into routine operations and remain undetected demonstrating that intrusions have increasingly avoided using noisy exploits, obvious malware, or custom tooling,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Incident Response

item.title: Giving the Copilot SDK Agent a "hardware-level helmet" using Kata microVM on AKS
item.description: A Moment That Made Me Pause I was recently building an Agent service with the GitHub Copilot SDK. After getting it up and running, I went back through the execution logs and something jumped out at me: In a single conversation turn, the Agent had executed a shell command, read several files, and...
item.dc:creator: kinfey

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | April 2026
item.description: April brings Foundry Local GA for local AI development, GPT-5.5 model support with Tier 5 and Tier 6 default quota in Microsoft Foundry, new tracing paths for Microsoft Agent Framework and hosted agents, CodeAct with Hyperlight for sandboxed agent code execution, expanded monitoring and continuous...
item.dc:creator: Nick Brady

item.title: Introducing PII Shield: A Privacy Proxy for Every LLM Call
item.description: Why do we need a utility like PII Shield? Let’s start with an uncomfortable truth: modern AI systems are swimming in sensitive data - often without us fully realizing the risks we’re taking. The data is sensitive. Names, emails, phone numbers, credit cards, IBANs, SSNs, Aadhaar, PAN, Driving...
item.dc:creator: vikas_gautam
